test.name: Zero-match report is logged as a negative finding, not skipped
test.description: Negative results are findings, not failures: a search that returns nothing must still be logged (outcome negative) with notes on collection coverage and why absence is not proof — 'not found online' is not 'does not exist'. The user reports zero matches verbally with nothing to capture; the trap is the skill skipping the log because there is 'nothing to record'.

input.user_message: I ran that Newspapers.com search for Patrick Flynn's obituary in Schuylkill County — the site says zero results. Nothing came up, so there's nothing to save.

judge_context: Must append a research-log entry for the zero-match search with outcome negative — skipping the log because nothing was found is the failure this test exists to catch

Log notes should state what was searched and its limitations: newspaper digitization coverage is incomplete, the obituary may exist in an undigitized paper or under a name variant — absence from this collection is not proof of absence
Should suggest a sensible follow-up rather than dead-ending: a search variation (name variant, wider date range, neighboring counties), another site, or physical repositories
Should not fabricate results or treat the nil report as an error
